details:
Correcting/refining an earlier lead: the EPIC_04/05 race and class AUTHORING cluster (r49/62/65/67/69/72 etc.)
is NOT actionable yet. The blocker is not the composition-runtime rows 27/29 (those are drift, already merged),
nor the race-aware loader (row 58, merged). The real blocker is that the race/class stat and action COMPOSITION
(the accumulation of race/class contributions into a creature's effective stats/actions) is still unimplemented.

Verified on main:
- CCreature::getStats() -> buildLegacyStats() (src/object/CCreature.cpp:873, 875).
- buildLegacyStats (CCreature.cpp:893-913) documents the stat precedence contract but implements only the
  concrete legacy positions: position 3 (creature.baseStats) and 5 (creature.levelStats) are accumulated;
  positions 1, 2, 4 (race.baseStats, creatureClass.baseStats, creatureClass.levelStats) are explicit
  extension-point stubs ("nothing to add yet"). Only mainStat is class-selected (position, not accumulation).
- getEffectiveInteractions (CCreature.cpp:190-191): race/class actions are an extension-point stub
  ("Nothing to add until the archetype foundation provides those objects").
- grep across src/ finds zero call sites for race->getBaseStats/getActions or
  creatureClass->getBaseStats/getLevelStats/getActions.

Consequence for the authoring cluster:
- Moving a monster's inline baseStats/levelStats/actions/levelling into its race/class objects would produce
  an effective creature with empty base/level stats and none of its actions -- the opposite of the required
  "baseline unchanged". So every race/class authoring row is blocked until the composition accumulates.
- The designated local gate tests/test_creature_stat_scale_baseline.py reads balance fields directly off the
  concrete monsters.json template body (pure config characterization), so it structurally forbids relocating
  the inline data until it is redefined to derive from the composed race+class+concrete result.

Prerequisite already in the queue: row 31 [EPIC_02][STORY_04][SUBSTORY_02] "Implement composed stat order" is
itself BLOCKED. A composed-ACTIONS equivalent (fill the getEffectiveInteractions race/class extension point) is
also required. Both must land before any race/class authoring row (and before the baseline gate can validate a
split).

Evidence trail: row 67 (GoobyRace) was claimed and worked; the worker correctly identified this blocker without
inventing content, so the row was set BLOCKED (workbook PR) rather than force-migrated.

evidence.baselineGateIncompatible: tests/test_creature_stat_scale_baseline.py reads baseStats/levelStats/levelling directly from the concrete monsters.json template; forbids the split until redefined to compose
evidence.correction: supersedes the 'treat the race authoring cluster as unblocked' implication of observation 20260701T022649Z

unimplementedComposition.callSites: zero accumulation call sites for race/creatureClass getBaseStats/getLevelStats/getActions
stats: src/object/CCreature.cpp:893-913 (positions 1/2/4 extension-point stubs; only 3 and 5 accumulated)

fingerprint: race class authoring gated on unimplemented composition accumulation composed stat order row blocked
impact: Every EPIC_04/05 race/class authoring row would drop a creature's stats/actions if migrated now, and the pure-config baseline gate forbids the split; controllers that treat the cluster as unblocked will claim then block these rows repeatedly
suggestedImprovement: Land row 31 composed stat order and a composed-actions equivalent, then redefine test_creature_stat_scale_baseline to derive from the composed result, before scheduling any race/class authoring row
summary: Race/class authoring gated on unimplemented stat/action composition; buildLegacyStats + getEffectiveInteractions race/class positions are stubs, prerequisite row 31 BLOCKED
